# CASE: infra-case-010-wsl-lan-bridge-runbook

WSL2 Docker dev stack healthy inside VM but `https://192.168.100.7` unreachable from Windows/LAN
after WSL or Docker restart — Windows↔WSL portproxy bridge drift, not app code.

Related: [[infra-case-006-dynamic-lan-host]]

## Vault links

- Registry: [[CASE_REGISTRY]] · Contracts: [[CONTRACTS_HUB]] · Home: [[OPERATOR_HOME]]
- Related cases: [[infra-case-006-dynamic-lan-host]]

## Problem

After `wsl --shutdown` or reboot, WSL2 VM IP changes. Stale `netsh portproxy` rules forward
`0.0.0.0:80/443/4443` to the old WSL address → browser "Site cannot be reached" at
`https://192.168.100.7` even when Docker/nginx are healthy inside WSL.

## Success Criterion

All seven operator checks pass; logon scheduled task registered; runbook documents daily
`pld sync` + when to run `pld network` manually.

## Specialist Investigation & Implementation

### Diagnostic evidence (2026-06-09)

| Check | Result |
| ----- | ------ |
| WSL `curl -sk https://localhost/login` | HTTP 200 |
| `openssl` SAN on `docker/certs/fullchain.pem` | `IP Address:192.168.100.7` present |
| WSL IP (`hostname -I`) | `172.21.115.211` |
| `netsh interface portproxy show v4tov4` | `0.0.0.0:80/443/4443 → 172.21.115.211` (matches WSL IP) |
| `curl.exe -ksf https://192.168.100.7/login` | HTTP 200 |
| `curl.exe -ksf https://192.168.100.7:4443/` | HTTP 200 |
| Scheduled task `Woosoo-WSL-LAN-Bridge` | State: Ready (not yet run since register — LastTaskResult 267011 = never executed) |
| Firewall `Woosoo-WSL-Dev` | Enabled, TCP 80/443/4443 inbound, Private+Public |

### Fix applied

**Fix A (portproxy)** — already correct at diagnosis time; no stale rules. Root cause class
confirmed: when IP drifts, `pld network` / `setup-wsl-lan-access.ps1` is the remedy.

**Fix B (certs)** — not needed; SAN includes `192.168.100.7`.

**Fix C (firewall)** — not needed; `Woosoo-WSL-Dev` rule present and enabled.

### Changes this session

1. **`scripts/windows/register-wsl-lan-startup-task.ps1`** — runs `setup-wsl-lan-access.ps1`
   immediately after task registration (elevated session already open).
2. **`docs/USAGE_GUIDE.md`** — new § WSL LAN bridge runbook (daily flow, one-time task, post-`wsl --shutdown`).
3. **`docs/deployment/DEPLOYMENT_GUIDE.md`** — runbook cross-link in §4.1.1.

Pre-existing (untracked, verified complete): `register-wsl-lan-startup-task.ps1`,
`unregister-wsl-lan-startup-task.ps1`, `setup-wsl-lan-access.ps1`.
